entries.title: BORED APE YACHT CLUB | Website Redesign

messages.title: Product Program Manager, Roadmap Planning & Program Management
messages.message:
Force multiplier who works quietly in the background while the roadmap stays on the rails — that's the seat I like. I've done a contract run as an Executive Producer at Airbnb, so the pace and the cross-functional scramble aren't news to me.

Most of my 15+ years has been standing between strategy and execution: Program Director at AKQA on Nike and Trek, Production Lead for Base at Coinbase, a $14MM retainer with a team of four. Bringing clarity out of chaos across design, eng, and marketing is the whole reason people call me mid-project.

Remote-first is my preference, so worth flagging the Bay Area requirement up front. Happy to talk if there's flexibility.

messages.title: Field Technical Program Manager
messages.message:
Straight up: this one's based in Korea and needs native Korean fluency — I've got the program chops but not the language, so I won't pretend otherwise.

Where I'd actually earn my keep is the AI-into-workflow piece. Most teams bolt AI on and the output scatters everywhere — product decisions in one doc, tasks with no context, conversations that go nowhere. I built an operating system for that on a small, confidential, aggressive-timeline app: source-of-truth structure and an orchestration agent that keeps AI-assisted work connected instead of shredded across ten tabs.

If the Korea requirement ever flexes remote, I'd love to talk.

insertedExample.oneHitter: Project Hub for Retrieval, Execution, & Delivery

messages.title: Process Strategy and Optimization Lead
messages.message: I've been on the inside at Airbnb before — Executive Producer on contract — so I know how much competing gravity a matrixed op like Fraud & Safety carries. The part of this role I'd own from day one: end-to-end process where the output goes everywhere. Product decisions in one doc, tasks with no context, AI conversations that lead nowhere. I built an operating system to fix exactly that on a small, high-stakes team — structure first, then acceleration, humans still in control of scope. Same instinct scales to hundreds of ops specialists. I'm good at making solutions stick, not just launch.

insertedExample.oneHitter: Project Hub for Retrieval, Execution, & Delivery

messages.title: Manager Business Process Improvement
messages.message:
The part of this that grabs me: engineering processes that are reliable, measurable, and can hand off to AI as capabilities evolve — not just optimizing what already exists. That's the exact problem I keep landing on. The output isn't the hard part; keeping it connected and self-correcting is.

I've built inside Airbnb before as an Executive Producer, and led production for Base at Coinbase — small teams, aggressive timelines, systems that had to hold up under scale. Most recently I built an operating methodology to keep AI-assisted work from scattering across a dozen docs, with humans owning the decisions that can't be undone.

Happy to walk through where I'd start.

insertedExample.oneHitter: Project Hub for Retrieval, Execution, & Delivery

messages.title: Manager, Luxe Supply
messages.message:
I've been inside the Airbnb machine before — Executive Producer on contract — so the pace and the cross-functional scramble aren't a surprise to me. What I do best is take a team, a mess of moving markets, and no obvious source of truth, and give it structure people actually follow. At AKQA I ran the Nike and Trek programs; at Swift I managed a $14MM HTC retainer with a team of four, hitting acquisition and quality targets without letting either slip.

Luxe supply across NAMER and LATAM is a lot of plates spinning at once. Coaching the market managers, owning the numbers, keeping quality sacred — that's the seat I'm built for. Happy to talk specifics.

messages.title: Senior Platform Manager, Trust and Safety
messages.message: I've done a lap through the Airbnb building already — Executive Producer on contract — so "trust is the currency" isn't a slogan I have to warm up to. Here's the honest read: my home turf is production and program leadership, not a T&S product roadmap. But the day-to-day of this role is exactly what I do — herding engineers, data scientists, designers, and policy folks toward a launch without letting the priorities scatter. I ran a $14MM retainer at Swift and won 4 new accounts as Program Director at AKQA by keeping cross-functional teams aligned when the stakes and the tradeoffs got ugly. If you want someone who can quietly hold that working group together, I'm worth a call.

messages.title: Group Product Manager, Core Infrastructure & Reliability
messages.message:
I have to be straight with you here: I'm a production and program leader, not a 10-year infrastructure PM. I'm not going to pretend I've been defining platform SLOs and multi-cloud strategy.

What I do know is Coinbase from the inside — I was Production Lead for Base. I know the intensity, the surges, the regulated-and-fast tension. I keep multidisciplinary teams pointed at the right thing without drama, and I've run AI-assisted workflows where the real problem was never output, it was orchestration — decisions and context scattering everywhere until nothing connects.

If there's a producer or program shape on the Platform side where that's useful, I'd love to talk. If this seat needs deep infra PM depth, no hard feelings.

messages.title: Art Director, Claude
messages.location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY
messages.message: Straight up: this Art Director role leans harder into hands-on visual craft than the producer/director hat I usually wear, so I won't pretend I'm the one sketching the concept on the whiteboard. What I am is the creative through-line between your team and outside agencies. At AKQA I ran Program Director on Nike and Trek, and I led both internal teams and the external studio Make Me Pulse on the Bored Ape Yacht Club redesign — brand context in, work stays yours, nothing gets lost between rooms. If you're shipping some campaigns in-house and steering others through partners, that seam is where I live. Worth a chat?

insertedExample.oneHitter: Yuga’s property Bored Ape Yacht Club was still rocking it’s original design and functionality in 2023.

workExampleSelection.title: BORED APE YACHT CLUB | Website Redesign

messages.title: Account Executive, Enterprise Hunter (French fluency)
messages.message: This one isn't a match, and I'd rather say so than waste your time. My 15+ years are in digital production and program leadership — running teams at AKQA, Coinbase, and Airbnb, managing a $14MM retainer, keeping multi-party projects from capsizing. Adjacent to enterprise sales, but not the same seat. I've sat across the table from C-suite stakeholders and led commercial negotiations on the delivery side, not carried a named-account quota with a French-fluency requirement. If a production or program lead ever opens up, I'd love to talk. Otherwise, best of luck filling this one.

messages.title: Software Engineer
messages.message: I'll be straight with you: I'm not shipping Golang PRs on your compliance platform. I ran production for Base at Coinbase, so I know the surge cadence and how the compliance side works. Where I earn my keep is the layer around the code — AI-first workflows that stay connected instead of scattered across a dozen docs. I built P.H.R.E.D. exactly for that: a source-of-truth system and an orchestration agent keeping AI-assisted work coherent while humans hold scope and privacy. If your CXAE team wants that structure alongside the engineers, that's my seat. If it's purely full-stack delivery, I'm probably the wrong boat.

insertedExample.oneHitter: Project Hub for Retrieval, Execution, & Delivery
